info.title: TatvaCare Partner API
info.description:
Push leads into TatvaCare and read their state back. Your key decides exactly which fields you can read and write, and which product line your leads belong to — there is no fixed payload.

## What this API does

Create or update leads by phone (idempotent), single or in bulk. Read one or list many. Delete by id. And **discover your schema** at runtime — call `lead_schema` to see exactly what your key may send and read.

## The shape

```
 Lead (one per phone, per product line + group)
   ├── top-level fields        — first_name, mobile_no, status, …
   └── child tables (arrays)   — e.g. custom_lab_profile[], custom_plan_profile[]
```

A lead is unique per phone + product line + group — re-sending the same patient updates that lead, it never duplicates, so every write is safe to retry. Product line and group are fixed to your key; the program is a mutable attribute (sent in *List* mode, stamped in *Forced* mode) and is never part of identity.

## Where to start

- **New here?** Read the [Overview](/partner-welcome) and [Quickstart](/partner-quickstart).
- **Building a request?** Call [`lead_schema`](/discover-schema) first — it returns your fields, which are required, and how your child tables work.
- **Reading a response?** See [Reading responses](/reading-values) and [Errors](/partner-errors).

Every endpoint has a live **try-it** console below. [Save your API key](/partner-validate-key) once — the console reuses it on every request — then open an endpoint and hit **Send**.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
Every request needs an `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: token <api_key>:<api_secret>
```

Literal lowercase `token`, a single space, then your key and secret joined by a colon.

[Save your API key](/partner-validate-key) once and the try-it console reuses it everywhere — you never re-paste it. Then use **Verify your API key** below to confirm the header is right before any lead call.

tags.name: Lead
tags.description:
Your entire surface — the gated lead methods. Each one resolves what your key is allowed to do: your fixed product line, and the fields you may send and read. Raw `/api/resource/CRM Lead` is **not** available to partner keys (it returns `403`); these methods are the supported path.

| HTTP method | Method | Does |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `lead_schema` | Discovery — exactly the fields YOUR key may send/read, plus your routing mode. |
| GET | `lead_get` | Read one lead by `name` or `mobile_no`. |
| POST | `lead_create` | Create-or-upsert by phone. Returns the CRM `name`. |
| PUT | `lead_update` | Update a lead by CRM `name` (the id `lead_create` returned). |
| DELETE | `lead_delete` | Delete a lead by CRM `name` (a lead with linked activity can't be deleted). |
| POST | `lead_create_bulk` | `{"leads":[{...}, …]}` (≤ 100). Partial success. |
| PUT | `lead_update_bulk` | `{"updates":[{"name":…, …}, …]}` (≤ 100). Partial success. |
| DELETE | `lead_delete_bulk` | `{"names":[…]}` (≤ 100). Partial success. |
| POST | `lead_get_bulk` | `{"names":[…]}` **or** `{"mobile_nos":[…]}` (≤ 100). Out-of-scope ids omitted. |
| GET | `lead_list` | Curated filters + pagination, always scoped to your line. |

**`lead_create` is the default write path.** It's idempotent on the lead grain, safe to retry, and handles the lookup-or-create for you.

### Lead grain and dedup

A lead is unique per **phone + product line + group** ([Lead identity](/concepts)). You never send routing — your line is fixed by your mapping — so for you, dedup is simply **phone within your line**: `lead_create` finds the lead matching your line and that phone and updates it, or creates a new one. The same phone on a different line is a separate lead.

### Child profile writes

Profile children (`custom_plan_profile`, `custom_lab_profile`) are sent as **embedded arrays** inside the body — at most one row per profile per lead. Writes **merge field-by-field** into the existing row: fields you send are overlaid, fields you omit are left alone. A partial write never wipes the row. This holds for `lead_create`, `lead_update`, and their bulk forms.

### Routing

Routing (`source`, line, group, program) is **forced server-side** from your key's mapping. Any routing field you send is silently ignored.

### Field validation

- **Allowed fields are per-key.** Call `lead_schema` for exactly what your key may send; anything outside that list is dropped. `mobile_no` is always allowed (the anchor).
- **Required to create:** `mobile_no`. `first_name` and `status` are default-filled if missing.
- **Required if status is Junk or Unqualified:** `lost_reason`.
- **Link-validated** (the value must exist in its master): `status`, `source`, program, and `substage` on the plan profile.
- **Never write:** `name` (auto-generated), `lead_name` (computed), `creation`, `modified`.

### Reading leads

Three paths: `lead_get` (one, by `name` or `mobile_no`), `lead_get_bulk` (many), `lead_list` (filtered + paginated). All return only your allowed fields and only leads on your line.

`lead_list` honors a **curated set of filters only** — never arbitrary fields:

| Parameter | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `status` | exact lead status |
| `created_after` / `created_before` | `creation` date range |
| `updated_after` / `updated_before` | `modified` date range |
| `mobile_no` | exact phone |
| `limit` | page size (default 20, max 200) |
| `offset` | rows to skip (paging) |

List results return curated parent fields, **no children** — use `lead_get` for the full record. Response: `{ok, total, count, offset, limit, leads:[…]}`.

### Bulk partial success

Every bulk method runs each record independently — one bad row reports its error, the rest still commit. Response: `{ok, total, succeeded, failed, results:[{index, ok, action?, name?, error?}]}`.

### Reading children

`lead_get` / `lead_get_bulk` return the profile children inline, filtered to your allowed fields. Child tables can't be read directly via REST — always read them through the parent lead.

---

To try any method below, [save your API key](/partner-validate-key) first — the try-it console then injects it on every request.

tags.name: Activities
tags.description:
Log **activities** on a lead — CRM Tasks of an activity type (a grain-scoped CRM Task Type). The SAME key that writes your leads writes activities: there is NO per-entity enablement, the single enabled `CRM Lead API Mapping` row + its grain governs all four entity APIs.

| HTTP method | Method | Does |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `activity_schema` | **Discovery BY LEAD** — the activity types available to a lead's grain, each with its field schema (what you may send for THIS patient). |
| GET | `activity_get` | Read one activity by CRM Task `name`. |
| POST | `activity_create` | Create-or-upsert (deduped on `external_id`). |
| PUT | `activity_update` | Re-run compute on an activity by `name`. |
| DELETE | `activity_delete` | Delete an activity by `name`. |
| POST | `activity_create_bulk` | `{"activities":[…]}` (≤ 100). Partial success. |
| POST | `activity_get_bulk` | `{"names":[…]}` **or** `{"external_ids":[…]}` (≤ 100). |
| GET | `activity_list` | By lead (+ optional `task_type`/`status`), paginated. |

### Discover before you send

Activity fields are **grain-scoped** — different leads expose different types and fields. Call `activity_schema?lead=<id>` (or `?mobile_no=`) first; send and read values **keyed by the schema `fieldname`** under `values`. Never hardcode a field list.

### Idempotency

`external_id` (stored in `custom_lsq_activity_id`) is the idempotency key: re-sending the same `external_id` updates that activity, never doubles. Optional `created_at` backdates the activity for historical loads.

### Scope

Every activity resolves its lead through the shared grain-scoped resolver — an out-of-scope or missing lead returns the SAME generic not-found (no probing). Rate limit 100 req/60s; bulk ≤ 100 with per-row partial success.

tags.name: Files
tags.description:
Attach **files** to a lead (or a scoped activity task). The SAME key as your leads — no per-entity enablement, the single enabled `CRM Lead API Mapping` row + its grain governs files too.

| HTTP method | Method | Does |
|---|---|---|
| POST | `file_attach` | Download/decode bytes and attach to a lead (or a scoped task). |
| GET | `file_get` | Read one file's metadata + proxy url. |
| GET | `file_list` | A lead's files (optional `file_type`), paginated. |
| DELETE | `file_delete` | Delete a file by `name`. |
| POST | `file_attach_bulk` | `{"files":[…]}` (≤ 100). Partial success. |

### Always private

**Every file attached via this API is private** (fail-closed privacy) — `is_private` is always `true` and `file_url` is a proxy URL, never a raw blob key or local path. Send the bytes as a downloadable `file_url` (the server fetches it) OR `content_base64` (exactly one).

### Idempotency

`external_id` (stored in `custom_lsq_attachment_id`) is the idempotency key: re-attaching the same `external_id` to the same record returns the existing file with `action: exists`, never a duplicate.

### Scope

The lead (or its task) is grain-scoped via the shared resolver — out-of-scope/missing is the SAME generic not-found. Rate limit 100 req/60s; bulk ≤ 100 partial success.

tags.name: Calls
tags.description:
Log **calls** on a lead — native `CRM Call Log` rows (the SAME store the telephony webhook writes). The SAME key as your leads — no per-entity enablement, the single enabled `CRM Lead API Mapping` row + its grain governs calls too.

| HTTP method | Method | Does |
|---|---|---|
| POST | `call_create` | Create-or-upsert a call log (deduped on `external_id`). |
| GET | `call_get` | Read one call by `name`. |
| GET | `call_list` | A lead's calls (+ optional `direction`/`status`), paginated. |
| DELETE | `call_delete` | Delete a call by `name`. |
| POST | `call_create_bulk` | `{"calls":[…]}` (≤ 100). Partial success. |

### Fail-closed attribution — read `lead`

Send `lead`/`mobile_no` to attach explicitly (grain-scoped). OMIT both and the server does a **STRICT** last-10-digit match on the customer number (`from_number` on Inbound, `to_number` on Outbound), scoped to your grain. **No match or an ambiguous (2+) match leaves the call UNLINKED — `lead` comes back `null`** — never attached to a wrong lead. **Always read `lead`** to know whether the call was linked.

### Idempotency

`external_id` (stored in `custom_external_id`) is the idempotency key: re-sending the same `external_id` updates that call log, never doubles. The CRM Call Log autoname is `field:id`, so the row `name` is NOT your `external_id` (a synthetic `PARTNER-<external_id>` id is minted). Optional `started_at` backdates `creation` on insert.

### Scope

Reads/deletes are grain-scoped; an unlinked call is never visible to a partner. Rate limit 100 req/60s; bulk ≤ 100 partial success.

apiKeyAuth.name: Authorization
apiKeyAuth.description: Format: `token <api_key>:<api_secret>` — literal lowercase `token`, single space, key:secret joined by `:`. See Conventions for how to request a key and verify it.

CRMLeadUniversal.description: Universal fields on `CRM Lead` itself. The top-level fields your key may send/read live here; child tables embed as arrays. Call `lead_schema` to see exactly which fields your key is granted.

name.description: Frappe primary key, autonamed `CRM-LEAD-YYYY-NNNNN`. **Read-only** — never include in POST bodies (server generates).

first_name.description: First name. Set to `(no name)` if you genuinely don't have it (`lead_create` default-fills this if omitted).

mobile_no.description: **Required** — the dedup anchor (with product line + group). E.164 format: `+<country code><digits>`, no hyphens, no spaces.

phone.description: Secondary phone (optional). E.164 same as mobile_no.

custom_alternate_number.description: Alternate contact phone (optional). E.164 same as mobile_no.

status.description: **Required on create.** Universal lifecycle status. New leads start as `New`. Junk and Unqualified are 'Lost' type and require `lost_reason`. Read the allowed values from `lead_schema`.

source.description: Lead acquisition channel. Link to `CRM Lead Source`. Read the allowed values from `lead_schema`.

lead_owner.description: Email of the CRM user owning this lead. Link to `User`. **Only set if the email is a real Frappe user** — otherwise leave blank to avoid `LinkValidationError`.

lost_reason.description: **Required when `status` is `Junk` or `Unqualified`.** Link to `CRM Lost Reason`.

lost_notes.description: Free-text note explaining why the lead was lost.

custom_vertical.description: **Permission-locked** (`permlevel=1`). Top-level product line. Link to `CRM Vertical`. See `lead_schema`.

custom_group.description: **Permission-locked.** Sub-line under the vertical. Link to `CRM Group`.

custom_current_program.description: The PRIMARY program. Link to `CRM Program`. **`permlevel=0`** — rep-writable, so a rep can self-handoff a program within their line (only `custom_vertical` + `custom_group` are manager-locked at `permlevel=1`). Multi-program patients are supported via profile-row presence (see `lead_schema`).

custom_previous_program.description: **Permission-locked.** The lead's previous program if they transitioned. Link to `CRM Program`. Captures the LAST switch only — full history is in the (deferred) activity layer.

custom_origin_vertical.description: **Permission-locked. Write-once on capture, never updated.** Records the vertical the patient first entered through, even if their current vertical changes later.

custom_source_origin.description: Where the lead source data originated (deprecated for new writes — use `source` instead).

custom_patient_id.description: External patient identifier from the source system (e.g. Product Line C user ID, partner system ID).

custom_lsq_prospect_id.description: Legacy LeadSquared ProspectId — migration-only dedup key. Not populated for new leads after the LSQ cutover.

custom_lead_gen_id.description: External lead-gen system identifier (campaign or form submission ID).

custom_patient_age.description: Patient age in years. Not auto-calculated from DOB today — caller-provided.

custom_caregiver_relation.description: Caregiver's relationship to patient (e.g. Spouse, Daughter, Son).

custom_plan_profile.description: **Embedded child rows** (max 1 today). Plan / PSP / subscription / drug-program / payment / acquisition / dx-funnel data. On PUT, the array REPLACES the existing rows — see `lead_schema`.

custom_lab_profile.description: Embedded child rows. Lab values, vitals, conditions, scores.

custom_care_providers_profile.description: Embedded child rows. Care team — coaches, RM, therapist, doctor, hospital.

modified.description: **Read-only.** Last-modified timestamp. Useful for change-tracking / delta sync.

PlanProfile.description: A plan / subscription / program profile row, sent as a JSON array under `custom_plan_profile`. The exact fields your key may send and read are **key-specific** — discover them via `lead_schema` (`data.children.custom_plan_profile.fields`). Never hardcode a field list.

name.description: The row's unique ID (read-only; present when you read a lead back).

LabProfile.description: A lab-values & vitals profile row, sent as a JSON array under `custom_lab_profile`. The exact fields your key may send and read are **key-specific** — discover them via `lead_schema` (`data.children.custom_lab_profile.fields`). Never hardcode a field list.

name.description: The row's unique ID (read-only; present when you read a lead back).

CareProvidersProfile.description: A care-team & providers profile row, sent as a JSON array under `custom_care_providers_profile`. The exact fields your key may send and read are **key-specific** — discover them via `lead_schema` (`data.children.custom_care_providers_profile.fields`). Never hardcode a field list.

name.description: The row's unique ID (read-only; present when you read a lead back).

UpsertLeadRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/method/tatva_connect.api.partner.lead_create`. Only `mobile_no` is strictly required (the dedup anchor); `first_name` and `status` are default-filled if missing (`(no name)` and `New`). All other fields are optional and must be within the fields your key is allowed to send (call `lead_schema` to see them). You never send routing — it's set for you server-side. Profile children embed as arrays and merge field-by-field.

LeadUpdateRequest.description: Request body for `lead_update`. Carries the CRM `name` (the id `lead_create` returned) plus any allowed fields to change. Parent fields you SEND are updated, fields you OMIT are left alone; profile children merge field-by-field. You can't change routing — it's fixed to your key.

PartnerError.description: Every failure returns this one envelope — even framework errors (bad key, malformed body) are normalized, so you never see a raw traceback. Branch on `error.code` (stable, machine-readable), show `error.message` to a human, and the HTTP status mirrors the code. Full `error.code` → HTTP map: see the Errors page.

code.description: Stable machine-readable code. Branch on this, not on the message text.

fields.description: Present on some `validation_error`s — the field name(s) that failed.

retry_after.description: Present only on `rate_limited` (429): seconds until your key's budget resets.

PartnerWriteResponse.description: Success envelope from `lead_create` / `lead_update` / `lead_delete`. **Top-level — there is no `message` wrapper** (the gated partner methods emit the body directly). Read `status` first, then `action`, then `data`. A failure instead returns the PartnerError envelope with a non-2xx status.

action.description: What happened: a new lead, an upsert onto an existing one, or a delete.

name.description: CRM Lead id (e.g. `CRM-LEAD-2026-00123`). PUT this back to `lead_update`.

source.description: Lead source (forced from your mapping for partners).

PartnerReadResponse.description: Success envelope from `lead_get` / `lead_list`. Top-level (no `message` wrapper). `action` is `fetched`; `data` carries the curated lead (`lead_get`) or the page object (`lead_list`).

data.description: The curated lead (`lead_get`) or the page object `{total,count,offset,limit,leads[]}` (`lead_list`).

PartnerSchemaResponse.description: Success envelope from `lead_schema`. Top-level (no `message` wrapper), no `action`. `data` holds the fields THIS key may send/read, the child-write rules, dedup grain, bulk limits, and routing mode.

children.description: Per child table: `{multi_row, key_field, fields[]}`. `multi_row:true` ⇒ upsert-by-key on `key_field`.

child_write.description: How to send child arrays + the upsert-by-key rule.

dedup.description: The lead grain (mobile_no + product line + group).

routing.description: `forced` / `list` / `none` for a partner (line + group always fixed); `caller-supplied` for a trusted internal caller.

PartnerBulkResultEntry.description: One record's outcome in a bulk response. `status` is `success` or `error` per row. On `success`: `action`+`name` (writes) or `data` (lead_get_bulk). On `error`: the same `error:{code,message}` block as the top-level PartnerError.

index.description: 0-based position in the request array (results are input-ordered).

action.description: Present on a successful write row.

name.description: CRM Lead id (present on a successful write row).

data.description: Curated lead (present on a successful `lead_get_bulk` row).

error.description: Present on a failed row (its savepoint rolled back).

PartnerBulkResponse.description: Partial-success envelope shared by every bulk method. Top-level (no `message` wrapper). Each record runs in its own savepoint — one bad row reports its error, the rest still commit. `summary` is `{total,succeeded,failed}` for writes and `{requested,found,not_found}` for `lead_get_bulk`. Inspect `results[].status` per row; the HTTP status is 200 even when some rows failed.

status.description: `success` — the batch ran (individual rows may have failed).

ActivitySchemaField.description: One field on an activity type, as returned by `activity_schema`. Send and read activity values keyed by `fieldname` (never hardcode a list — discover them per type).

fieldname.description: The key to send/read this value under inside the activity `values` object.

options.description: For `Link` the target doctype; for `Select` the newline-separated options; else null.

ActivityType.description: One grain-scoped activity type available to a lead, returned by `activity_schema`. Pick a `name` and send its `fields` under `values`.

name.description: The activity type id (a CRM Task Type) — send it as `task_type` on create/update.

is_logged_complete.description: `1` ⇒ logging this activity marks it complete (it carries no separate status workflow).

fields.description: The fields an integrator may send under `values` for this type.

Activity.description: The partner-facing shape of an activity (a CRM Task of an activity type). `values` is re-keyed to the type's schema `fieldname`s — read/write activity data through `values`, never raw CRM Task columns.

name.description: The CRM Task id — the activity's unique id (PUT/DELETE this back).

lead.description: The CRM Lead this activity hangs off.

task_type.description: The activity type (a CRM Task Type from `activity_schema`).

external_id.description: **Idempotency key** (stored in `custom_lsq_activity_id`). Re-sending the same `external_id` updates that activity, never doubles.

values.description: The activity's data, keyed by the type's schema `fieldname`s (discover them via `activity_schema`).

ActivityUpsertRequest.description: Body for `activity_create` / `activity_update`. Target a lead by `lead` (CRM name) OR `mobile_no`. `task_type` names the activity type; `values` carries data keyed by the type's `fieldname`s (from `activity_schema`). `external_id` is the idempotency key — re-send to update, never doubles.

lead.description: CRM Lead id. Send this OR `mobile_no`.

external_id.description: Idempotency key (stored in `custom_lsq_activity_id`). Re-sending the same value updates that activity instead of creating a new one.

values.description: Activity data keyed by the type's schema `fieldname`s. Required fields per type come from `activity_schema`.

created_at.description: Optional. Backdates the activity's `creation` (historical / migration load). Ignored on update.

name.description: **`activity_update` only** — the CRM Task id to update.

File.description: The partner-facing shape of a file on a lead (or a scoped activity task). Files are **always private** (Invariant: fail-closed privacy) — `file_url` is a proxy URL, never a raw blob/local path.

name.description: The File id — the file's unique id (DELETE this back).

file_type.description: The document category (stored in `custom_file_type`), e.g. `Lab Report`, `Prescription` — caller-supplied.

external_id.description: **Idempotency key** (stored in `custom_lsq_attachment_id`). Re-attaching the same `external_id` to the same record returns the existing file (`action: exists`).

file_url.description: Proxy URL to fetch the (private) bytes — never a raw blob key or local path.

is_private.description: Always `true` — files attached via this API are private by design.

attached_to_doctype.description: What the file hangs off — the lead, or a scoped activity task.

attached_to_name.description: The id of the lead or task the file is attached to.

FileAttachRequest.description: Body for `file_attach`. Target a lead by `lead` OR `mobile_no` (optionally a scoped `activity` task). Supply the bytes as a downloadable `file_url` OR `content_base64` (exactly one). Idempotent on `external_id` + the target record.

lead.description: CRM Lead id. Send this OR `mobile_no`.

activity.description: Optional CRM Task id to attach to instead of the lead. The task MUST belong to this lead (else generic not-found).

external_id.description: **Required.** Idempotency key (stored in `custom_lsq_attachment_id`). Re-attaching the same value to the same record returns the existing file (`action: exists`).

file_url.description: A (signed / expiring) URL the server downloads the bytes from. Send this OR `content_base64`.

content_base64.description: The file bytes, base64-encoded. Send this OR `file_url`.

Call.description: The partner-facing shape of a call log (a native CRM Call Log row). **`lead` may be `null`** when no phone match or an ambiguous (2+) match was found — fail-closed attribution never attaches a call to the wrong lead. Always read `lead` to know whether the call was linked.

name.description: The CRM Call Log id (the autonamed row name; NOT the `external_id`).

external_id.description: **Idempotency key** (stored in `custom_external_id`). Re-sending the same value updates that call log, never doubles.

lead.description: The linked CRM Lead — **`null` when attribution found no/ambiguous match** (the call is left unlinked, never attached to a wrong lead). Read this.

direction.description: Call direction (maps to the CRM Call Log `type`: Inbound⇒Incoming, Outbound⇒Outgoing).

recording_url.description: URL of the call recording, if any.

CallUpsertRequest.description: Body for `call_create`. `external_id` (idempotency key) and `direction` are required. Attribution: send `lead`/`mobile_no` to target a lead explicitly (grain-scoped); OMIT both to let the server STRICT-match the customer number (`from_number` on Inbound, `to_number` on Outbound) within your grain — no/ambiguous match leaves the call **unlinked** (`lead: null`), never a wrong lead.

external_id.description: **Required.** Idempotency key (stored in `custom_external_id`). Re-sending the same value updates that call log, never doubles.

lead.description: Optional CRM Lead id to attach to (grain-scoped). If sent with `mobile_no`, `lead` wins.

mobile_no.description: Optional lead phone (auto-normalized) to attach to (grain-scoped).

from_number.description: Calling party number. On an Inbound call with no `lead`/`mobile_no`, this is the customer number used for attribution.

to_number.description: Called party number. On an Outbound call with no `lead`/`mobile_no`, this is the customer number used for attribution.

status.description: Call status. Defaults to `Completed` on insert if omitted.

started_at.description: Call start time. On insert it also backdates the row's `creation` (historical load).

EntityListResponse.description: Success envelope from the entity list endpoints (`activity_list` / `file_list` / `call_list`). Top-level (no `message` wrapper), `action: fetched`. `data` is a page object with `total/count/offset/limit/has_more` plus the entity array (`activities` / `files` / `calls`).

total.description: Total rows matching the filter (across all pages).

data.description: Page object; the entity array is keyed `activities` / `files` / `calls` depending on the endpoint.

EntityReadResponse.description: Success envelope from the entity single-read / write endpoints (`*_get`, `activity_create/update`, `file_attach`, `call_create`, `activity_schema`). Top-level (no `message` wrapper). `action` reflects what happened; `data` is the entity (Activity / File / Call) or the schema/discovery object.

data.description: The entity (Activity / File / Call), the `{name}` on a delete, or the discovery object on `activity_schema`.

EntityGetBulkResponse.description: Partial-success envelope for `activity_get_bulk`. Top-level (no `message` wrapper). `summary` is `{requested, found, not_found}`; `results[]` is input-ordered — `results[i]` is the i-th requested id. A found row carries `data` (the entity); a missing/out-of-scope id reports `not_found` in place.

data.description: The entity (present on a found row).

MinimalUpsert.summary: Minimum viable — just enough to create a lead
MinimalUpsert.description: Only the dedup key (`mobile_no`) plus mandatory `first_name`/`status` (or let them default-fill). No routing, no profiles. Useful for an absolute-minimum smoke test.

UpsertWithOneProfile.summary: Add one profile child — lab values for an insurer lead
UpsertWithOneProfile.description: Now we attach a Lab Profile row. Typical of an insurer pushing a lab-screened lead.

InsurerLabIntakeLead.summary: Full — insurer-screening lead (labs + policy + alt phone)
InsurerLabIntakeLead.description: Realistic shape for an inbound insurer feed. Plan Profile for policy/payment, Lab Profile for the full panel.

OncologyPSPLead.summary: Full — oncology PSP lead (drug program + care team)
OncologyPSPLead.description: Illustrative shape for a Group A / Program B clinical lead. The Plan Profile fields shown are examples only — your key's actual fields come from `lead_schema`. Care & Providers carries the assigned coach, doctor, and hospital.

WellnessSubscriptionLead.summary: Full — wellness subscription lead (plan + score + care team)
WellnessSubscriptionLead.description: Realistic shape for a Product Line C-style subscription sign-up. Plan Profile carries subscription + UTM; Lab Profile carries metabolic score; Care & Providers carries the assigned coach stack.

UpsertSuccessUpdated.summary: lead_create response — updated (existing lead found on the same line)

LeadSchemaResponse.summary: lead_schema — a key granted labs + plan fields

data.child_write: Send each child as a JSON array under its key. Multi-row children are upsert-by-key on key_field: a new key adds a row, the same key updates that row (only sent fields change), omitted rows are untouched. Single-row children merge onto the one row. Delete a row with {<key_field>, "_delete": true}.
data.dedup: A lead is unique per (mobile_no, product line, group). Re-sending the same patient updates that lead. Program is NOT part of identity: sending the same patient with a different program transitions the SAME lead, never a new one.

routing.note: Your routing is fixed. Any routing fields you send are ignored.

CuratedLeadGet.summary: lead_get — one curated lead with children

LeadUpdateRequest.summary: lead_update — flip status on a known lead

value.name: CRM-LEAD-2026-00123
value.summary: lead_update — flip status, upsert one lab row by its key

LeadListResponse.summary: lead_list — page of curated leads (no children)

ActivitySchemaResponse.summary: activity_schema — types available to a lead's grain

ActivityCreateRequest.summary: activity_create — log a welcome call (deduped on external_id)

ActivityListResponse.summary: activity_list — page of a lead's activities

CallCreateRequestAttribute.summary: call_create — no lead, server strict-matches the customer number

CallCreateResponseUnlinked.summary: call_create — created but UNLINKED (no/ambiguous phone match)
CallCreateResponseUnlinked.description: Attribution found no single matching lead in your grain, so `lead` is `null`. The call is stored but not attached to any lead — read `lead` to detect this.

get.description: Returns the exact fields **your key** may send and read, which are required, your routing mode, and how each child table works — call this instead of hardcoding a field list, since two keys get different schemas. Full walkthrough: [Schema discovery](/discover-schema).

200.description: The caller's allowed fields + routing mode under `data` (no `message` wrapper).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

get.description: Fetch one lead by `name` — its unique ID, e.g. `CRM-LEAD-2026-00123` — or by `mobile_no`. Returns your allowed fields plus read-only routing, with child profiles inlined. You only see leads on your own line; an out-of-scope lookup returns `404 not_found`.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The lead's unique ID, e.g. `CRM-LEAD-2026-00123` (returned by Create Lead). Use this OR `mobile_no`.

parameters.name: mobile_no
parameters.description: 10-digit Indian number (auto-normalized to `+91…`) or an already-`+`-prefixed number. Use this OR `name`.

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call, or the lead is outside your line.

404.description: Lead not found or outside your line (identical — anti-enumeration).

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

get.description: List leads on your line with a curated set of safe filters, paginated (page size defaults to 20, max 200). Returns curated parent fields only — **no children**; use Get Lead Data for a full record.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Rows to skip (paging). Alias `limit_start` is also accepted.

200.description: A page of curated leads under `data` (no children — use `lead_get` for the full record).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

post.description: The default write for most integrations. Idempotent on lead identity (`mobile_no` + product line + group): it updates the matching lead or creates a new one, so it is always safe to retry. Returns `name`, the lead's unique ID. Identity model: [Concepts](/concepts).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted, or the lead is outside your line.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

post.description: Create or update up to 100 leads in one call — body `{"leads": [ ... ]}`. Same idempotent identity and field rules as Create Lead; each row commits in its own savepoint, so one bad row reports its error while the rest still commit (**partial success**). Over 100 rows is rejected.

leads.description: Up to 100 lead payloads (same shape as `lead_create`).

200.description: Batch ran — partial success. Inspect `summary` + `results[].status` (HTTP 200 even if some rows failed).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

post.description: Read up to 100 leads in one call by `names` or `mobile_nos` (send one). Returns the leads found — allowed fields, read-only routing, inlined children; out-of-scope or missing ids are omitted, so `found` may be less than `requested`. POST because the id list travels in the body.

names.description: Up to 100 lead IDs (e.g. `CRM-LEAD-2026-00123`). Use this OR `mobile_nos`.

mobile_nos.description: Up to 100 phone numbers (auto-normalized). Use this OR `names`.

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

put.description: Update a lead by `name` — its unique ID, e.g. `CRM-LEAD-2026-00123`. Fields you send are updated, fields you omit are left alone, and child profiles merge field-by-field, so a partial write never wipes a row. You can only update leads on your own line.

403.description: Your key isn't permitted, or the lead is outside your line.

404.description: Lead not found or outside your line (identical — anti-enumeration).

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

put.description: Update up to 100 leads in one call — body `{"updates": [ {"name": ..., ...} ]}`. Each entry needs the lead's `name` plus the fields to change; same merge semantics and line scope as Update Lead, per record in its own savepoint (**partial success**).

updates.description: Up to 100 update payloads; each carries `name` + allowed fields.

200.description: Batch ran — partial success. Inspect `summary` + `results[].status` (HTTP 200 even if some rows failed).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted, or a record is outside your line.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

delete.description: Delete a lead by `name` (its unique ID), in the query or body. You can only delete leads on your own line. A lead with linked activity (calls, tasks, notes) cannot be deleted and returns `cannot_delete` — see [Errors](/partner-errors).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The lead's unique ID, e.g. `CRM-LEAD-2026-00123`, to delete (may also be sent in the JSON body).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted, or the lead is outside your line.

404.description: Lead not found or outside your line (identical — anti-enumeration).

409.description: Lead has linked activity — `cannot_delete` (generic message by design).

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

delete.description: Delete up to 100 leads by `name` — body `{"names": [ ... ]}`. Each delete runs in its own savepoint (**partial success**) and is line-scoped; a lead with linked activity fails for that row while the rest proceed.

names.description: Up to 100 lead IDs (e.g. `CRM-LEAD-2026-00123`) to delete.

200.description: Batch ran — partial success. Inspect `summary` + `results[].status` (HTTP 200 even if some rows failed).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted, or a record is outside your line.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

error.message: Rate limit exceeded (100 requests/min). Retry in 42s.

get.summary: Activity Schema (discover types for a lead)
get.description: **Discovery by lead.** Given `?lead=<id>` or `?mobile_no=`, returns the activity types available to that lead's grain, each with its field schema — exactly what an integrator may send for this patient. Send and read activity values keyed by the schema `fieldname`. Call this before building an activity payload; never hardcode a field list.

parameters.name: lead
parameters.description: CRM Lead id. Use this OR `mobile_no`.

200.description: The activity types + field schemas available to this lead's grain.

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

404.description: Lead not found or outside your line (identical — anti-enumeration).

post.description: Attach a file to a lead (or a scoped activity task). Supply the bytes as a downloadable `file_url` (the server fetches it) OR `content_base64` (exactly one). **The file is always stored private.** Idempotent on `external_id` (stored in `custom_lsq_attachment_id`): re-attaching the same value to the same record returns the existing file with `action: exists`.

200.description: File attached (`action: attached`) or already present (`action: exists`).

post.description:
Create-or-upsert a call log. `external_id` and `direction` are required. Idempotent on `external_id` (stored in `custom_external_id`): re-sending updates the same row, never doubles.

**Attribution is fail-closed — read `lead`.** Send `lead`/`mobile_no` to attach explicitly (grain-scoped); OMIT both to let the server STRICT-match the customer number (`from_number` on Inbound, `to_number` on Outbound) within your grain. No match or an ambiguous (2+) match leaves the call **unlinked** — `lead` comes back `null` — never attached to a wrong lead.

200.description: Call created or updated. **Read `data.lead`** — it is `null` when attribution found no/ambiguous match (the call is unlinked).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

404.description: An explicitly-sent lead/mobile_no was not found or is outside your line (identical — anti-enumeration). Server-attribution misses do NOT 404; they return `lead: null`.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

get.description: Read one call by `name`, grain-scoped (own line only). An UNLINKED call is never visible to a partner. Out-of-scope/missing ⇒ the SAME generic not-found.

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

404.description: Call not found or outside your line (identical — anti-enumeration).

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

post.description: Create-or-upsert up to 100 call logs — body `{"calls": [ … ]}`. Same idempotent (`external_id`) and fail-closed attribution rules as Create Call; each row runs in its own savepoint (**partial success**). A row may succeed yet still be unlinked (`lead: null`) — but the bulk result reports `name`/`external_id`, not `lead`; read each back via `call_get`/`call_list` to confirm linkage.

calls.description: Up to 100 call payloads (same shape as `call_create`).

200.description: Batch ran — partial success. Inspect `summary` + `results[].status` (HTTP 200 even if some rows failed).

403.description: Your key isn't permitted to make this call.

429.description: Over 100 requests/60s for your key. `error.retry_after` = seconds to wait.

get.description: Confirms your `Authorization` header is correct — it returns the account your key belongs to. Make it your first call when integrating, and a health-check in CI: anything other than `200` means your auth is wrong. Setup: [Authentication](/partner-validate-key).
